description_long: This is a rustic log cabin constructed primarily from horizontally stacked, round wooden logs with visible notched corners. The roof is a steeply pitched gable style, covered with wide, weathered wooden planks that extend well beyond the walls, providing significant overhangs on both sides. The front facade features a small, centered wooden door and a triangular log pattern above the entrance, while the rear and side walls are solid with no visible windows or openings. The structure sits on a flat, green base, suggesting placement in a natural or park-like urban setting. The logs are dark brown with a natural, rough texture, and the roof planks are slightly lighter in color, showing signs of age and exposure. The overall appearance is robust and traditional, evoking classic North American or Scandinavian log cabin architecture.
description: A traditional log cabin with a steep gable roof, made from stacked round logs and weathered wooden planks, featuring a small front door and no visible windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the centered wooden door, triangular log pattern above the entrance, and the deep roof overhang.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the solid log wall and the full length of the overhanging gable roof, with no windows or doors visible.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a completely solid log wall with no openings and the continuation of the gable roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the solid log wall and roof overhang without any windows or doors.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with wide overhangs, constructed from weathered wooden planks
attribute_window_arrangement: No windows are visible on any side; the cabin appears to have solid log walls throughout.
attribute_door_placement: A single wooden door is centered on the front facade beneath the gable.
